preamble: The shift from the lateral approximant [l] to the mid-front vowel [e] is a form of L-vocalization. While vocalization typically results in back vowels like [u] or [o], in certain Central Germanic dialects, syllable-final [l] shifts toward a fronted vocoid or glide, particularly after other front vowels.

examples.note: The lateral vocalizes into a front glide or vowel quality.

sources: Waterman: A History of the German Language
